name: Black Bean Lasagna
instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and position the rack in the center. Lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with cooking spray or a thin layer of oil to prevent sticking. 

2. Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering. Add the diced onion and bell peppers, cooking for 3-4 minutes until they begin to soften. 

3. Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until fragrant. 

4. Stir in the black beans (drained and rinsed), diced tomatoes with their juice, and salsa. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. 

5. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to medium-low. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the vegetables are tender. The mixture should be thick enough to hold its shape when layered. 

6. While the bean mixture simmers, prepare the avocado layer: In a medium bowl, mash the avocado with the remaining garlic and lemon juice until smooth and creamy. Season with a pinch of salt if desired. 

7. To assemble the lasagna, start by placing a layer of corn tortillas on the bottom of the prepared baking dish, overlapping slightly to cover the entire surface. You may need to tear some tortillas to fit. 

8. Spread one-third of the bean mixture evenly over the tortillas, making sure to reach all the edges. 

9. Spread half of the avocado mixture over the bean layer, then sprinkle with one-third of the shredded cheddar cheese. 

10. Add another layer of tortillas, pressing down gently to compact the layers. 

11. Spread half of the remaining bean mixture over the tortillas, followed by the remaining avocado mixture. Sprinkle with half of the remaining cheese. 

12. Add a final layer of tortillas, then top with the remaining bean mixture. Spread it evenly and sprinkle with the remaining cheese, covering the entire surface. 

13. Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil (spray the underside with cooking spray to prevent cheese from sticking) and bake for 25 minutes. 

14. Remove the foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the edges are lightly golden. 

15. Let the lasagna rest for 5-10 minutes before cutting to allow the layers to set. This makes it easier to serve neat portions. Garnish with fresh cilantro, sour cream, or additional salsa if desired.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and grease a baking dish. 

2. Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion, bell peppers, and garlic; cook until softened. 

3. Stir in black beans, tomatoes, and salsa. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until thickened. 

4. Mash avocado with remaining garlic and lemon juice in a bowl. 

5. Layer tortillas in the baking dish, then 1/3 of bean mixture, 1/2 of avocado mixture, and 1/3 of cheese. 

6. Repeat with another layer of tortillas, 1/2 remaining bean mixture, remaining avocado mixture, and 1/2 remaining cheese. 

7. Add final layer of tortillas, remaining bean mixture, and remaining cheese. 

8. Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake 10 more minutes. 

9. Let rest 5-10 minutes before serving.
